body:0001.text: This article is machine translated from english

body:0003.text: Every project has a timeline, and this timeline is made up of different phases. Each phase is defined by a specific participation method.

body:0004.text: While the project itself sets the overall scope, visibility, and high-level overview, the phase is where you configure the details for each step. Because each step in a project can be quite different, phases have their own settings and specifications depending on the chosen participation method.

body:0005.text: ℹ️ You can learn more about these options in the Phase Settings section below, or by checking our Overview of Our Participation Methods article.

body:0006.text: What are the different phase settings?

body:0007.text: Each project contains a timeline divided into phases. Each phase has its own settings, depending on the participation method used.

body:0010.text: The Setup section defines the default settings for a phase. Each participation method has unique settings. All phase settings can be found in the Setup tab of each phase.

body:0011.text: Required fields: Phase title and phase dates

body:0012.text: Phase dates: Determine the date and time the phase starts and ends. Leaving the end date empty makes the phase open-ended; when you later add another phase, the platform automatically assigns an end date to keep the timeline consistent. Phase cannot overlap, however you phases can end and start on the same day as long as the times don’t overlap.

body:0013.text: Tooltips: Each setting includes a tooltip with more details on what the setting achieves

body:0015.text: Here is where you will be able to set your phase description.

body:0016.text: you can publish what you have written or keep some information in draft to publish at a later date

body:0017.text: The edits are saved even if you go away from the page, the edits just won’t appear publicly until you publish.

body:0020.text: The Input Manager allows you to view all inputs related to the current phase and manage their availability across other phases within the same project.

body:0021.text: ℹ️ The Input Manager is a powerful feature with multiple uses. To explore its full capabilities, refer to one of the articles below. Each dives deeper into specific aspects of how the Input Manager works:

body:0025.text: Tag, copy and move inputs

body:0026.text: Assigning input and ideas to your colleagues

body:0027.text: Providing feedback and managing inputs

body:0030.text: In this section you can edit the form that users have to fill in when they want to participate in the phase.

body:0031.text: ℹ️ For more information on the specific participation methods, navigate to their specific article where they are explained in more depth:

body:0038.text: If you want to use maps in your phase, this is the place where you can configure your map.

body:0039.text: ℹ️ For more information on maps, read our articles on Map Consultations and Using ESRI ArcGIS maps.

body:0042.text: Phase-level rights define who can participate in each phase action (posting input, commenting, voting, completing surveys, etc.). You can also choose to ask extra demographic questions on the phase level.

body:0043.text: ℹ️ For more information on phase-level access rights, read our article on Understanding Access Rights

body:0046.text: Our platform uses automatic emails to be sent out to users, these are also referred to as notifications. Certain notifications can be customized on a phase-level, you can do so in this tab.

body:0047.text: ℹ️ For more information on automated emails read our article on automated email notifications.

body:0050.text: In this section you can create a report and choose to make it visible or not to end-users.

body:0051.text: ℹ️ For more information on our Report Builder feature read our support article Creating and building reports with the Report Builder

body:0054.text: In this section you can manage your volunteering phase.

body:0055.text: ℹ️ For more information read our article on Organizing a volunteering project

body:0056.text: Phase data: Understanding the Insights phase tab

body:0058.text: The Insights tab allows you to view and understand the results of a phase.

body:0059.text: It brings together participation data, audience information, and phase-specific results in one place, helping you interpret how people engaged with your participation method.

body:0060.text: Rather than reviewing individual inputs one by one, the Insights tab provides a structured overview that makes it easier to:

body:0062.text: See who participated and how representative the audience is

body:0063.text: Analyze results based on the participation method used

body:0064.text: For reporting purposes, the Insights tab can also be exported to a PDF or Word, allowing you to easily share results with internal or external stakeholders.

body:0065.text: The Insights tab is available for phases that allow participant interaction and can include input from both logged-in and logged-out participants, depending on the access rights of the phase.

body:0066.text: What information is in the insights tab?

body:0068.text: The Insights tab is divided into three main sections:

body:0069.text: High-level statistics, providing an overall summary of participation

body:0070.text: Demographic & Audience information for the phase, offering insight into who participated

body:0071.text: Phase-specific results, depending on the participation method used

body:0072.text: Each section is explained in more detail below.

body:0074.text: This section provides an aggregated overview of how your phase performed.

body:0075.text: Depending on the participation method, the following data points may be shown:

body:0077.text: The number of people who viewed your phase. This includes both logged-in and logged-out visitors.

body:0079.text: The number of people who actively participated in the phase. Depending on access rights, this may include both logged-in and logged-out participants.

body:0081.text: The total number of responses or ideas submitted.

body:0083.text: The percentage of participants who started the survey and completed it.

body:0085.text: The number of comments added to submitted inputs.

body:0087.text: The number of likes or dislikes given to inputs or comments.

body:0089.text: A graph comparing visitors and participants over a selected time period, helping you understand how engagement evolved throughout the phase.

body:0090.text: Demographic & Audience information for the phase

body:0091.text: The platform allows you to collect demographic information in two ways:

body:0092.text: Through registration questions when users create an account

body:0093.text: Through added demographic questions within a phase

body:0095.text: Setting up the registration process and registration questions

body:0097.text: In this section of the Insights tab, you can browse through the demographic questions that applied to the phase and view how different participant groups are represented. This helps you assess the representativeness of your results.

body:0099.text: This section displays insights that are specific to the participation method used in the phase.

body:0101.text: An ordered overview of all survey questions and the answers provided by participants

body:0103.text: A summary overview of submitted ideas

body:0104.text: A topic breakdown showing how ideas are distributed across topics

body:0105.text: A top 5 list of the most liked ideas

body:0106.text: A status breakdown showing the distribution of idea statuses

body:0108.text: A list of all items that could be voted on, ordered by number of votes

body:0109.text: 💡 Optionally, demographic clustering can be enabled to explore how different demographic groups voted

body:0110.text: Where to find the insights tab?

body:0111.text: You can find the Insights tab within a phase that supports participant interaction.

body:0112.text: Find your project in the backoffice of your platform

body:0113.text: In your project on the top tab select Timeline

body:0114.text: Click on the phase you want to explore your insights for

body:0116.text:
⚠️ If the Insights tab is not available for that phase, it means this was a phase without participant interaction.
​

body:0117.text: How to export the insights tab as PDF or Word?

body:0118.text: Find your project in the backoffice of your platform

body:0119.text: In your project on the top tab select Timeline

body:0120.text: Click on the phase you want to explore your insights for

body:0122.text: At the top right you see a Download button that exports a PDF or Word

body:0123.text: How to setup your project timeline in phases?

body:0124.text: By default your project will prompt you to create a first phase

body:0125.text: Choose a timeframe on which you want the phase to be active

body:0126.text: Choose your desired participation method for the phase

body:0127.text: Configure the settings for the phase

body:0128.text: Press the + New phase icon on the right of the timeline to add extra phases until you have your entire project outlined

body:0130.text: Navigate to the Project that contains the phase you want to delete

body:0131.text: Click on the phase in the timeline that you want to delete

body:0132.text: Below the timeline you’ll see the phase title, on the right side of the screen you’ll see 3 dots. Click these dots and press Delete phase

body:0135.text: Phases are tied directly to the project timeline, which means they cannot overlap or run in parallel within the same project. If you need multiple phases to run at the same time, you’ll need to create separate projects.

body:0136.text: While projects have a publication status (e.g. draft or published), phases do not. This means it's not possible to add a phase in draft mode, once it's created, it's carrying the publication status of the project.
